data.title: KKR to Acquire Controlling Stake in Indian Hospital Operator for $400 Million
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

KKR has agreed to buy a controlling stake in Indian hospital operator HealthCare Global Enterprises for $400 million, adding to its recent investments in the country's healthcare industry.

The U.S. investment company said Sunday that it would acquire a stake of up to 54% in HealthCare Global from CVC Capital Partners and assume sole control of its operations.

HealthCare Global founder BS Ajaikumar will become nonexecutive chairman, focusing on clinical, academic and research and development work, the companies said.

Founded in 1989, Bengaluru-based HealthCare Global is one of India's largest cancer-care providers, operating 25 medical-care centers.

Akshay Tanna, partner and head of India private equity at KKR, said its investment in HealthCare Global would support the development of medical infrastructure and the delivery of oncology services in the country. Healthcare continues to be a thematic focus for the firm in India, he said.

KKR recently invested in Baby Memorial Hospital in the southern Indian state of Kerala and Bengaluru-based medical devices company Healthium Medtech.

KKR said it would buy shares of HealthCare Global from CVC at 445 rupees, equivalent to $5.14, apiece. The U.S. investment company will also start an open offer to purchase additional shares in the hospital operator from public shareholders.

Upon completion of the offer, KKR is expected to hold an equity stake in HealthCare Global of between 54% and 77%, it said.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Singapore's Inflation Likely Picked Up in January, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's CPI likely rose 1.95% on year in January, according to the median estimate of six economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. That would compare with December's 1.6% increase. Headline inflation is expected to pick up due to a higher contribution from accommodation costs and private road transport, Sanjay Mathur, chief economist for Southeast Asia and India at ANZ, says in a note. Core inflation is projected to have eased to 1.6% last month from December's 1.8% rise, according to five of the economists polled. Core inflation will likely continue declining, partly due to last year's higher base from the GST hike, ANZ says. The data are due Monday. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: MISC Bhd.'s Seen as Defensive Pick Amid Value Emergence — Market Talk
data.description: MISC Bhd.'s appears to be a defensive pick, given its recurring and stable earnings and cash flows, Maybank IB analyst Jeremie Yap says in a note. He upgrades MISC's rating to buy from hold, citing its emerging value at current valuations and a decent dividend yield at 5.0%. However, the analyst expects persistently weak liquefied natural gas shipping rates to weigh on earnings due to vessel oversupply and delays in liquefaction plant construction. MISC plans to replace expiring contracts with new builds and add 11 new LNG vessels in 2026, Yap notes. He lowers MISC's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 3% and 4%, respectively, following an unexpected additional cost provision in 4Q 2024. Maybank raises MISC's target price to MYR8.34 from MYR8.09. Shares are 0.4% lower at MYR7.12. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: APAC Resources Swings to Attributable Loss in Fiscal H1
data.description:
APAC Resources swung to an attributable loss of HK$309.9 million in the fiscal first half from a profit of HK$593.3 million in the year-ago period, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The commodities traders swung to a loss per share of HK$0.228 in the half year ended Dec. 31, 2024 from earnings per share of HK$0.455 in the previous year.

Total revenue plunged to HK$147.8 million from HK$941.4 million a year earlier.

data.title: Svava Joins Race to Acquire SelfWealth With AU$0.28 Cash Per Share Bid, Shares Rise Above 3%
data.description:
SelfWealth received a binding proposal from Svava to acquire 100% of the shares in the firm for AU$0.28 cash per share by way of a scheme of arrangement, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The firm earlier entered into a scheme implementation deed with Bell Financial Group for an offer with a cash consideration of AU$0.25 per share.

After being provided with satisfactory information regarding Svava's funding capacity and regulatory approval requirements, the board of SelfWealth decided to begin the matching right process under the deed with Bell Financial.

Bell Financial has on Feb. 26 to provide a counterproposal in response to the Svava proposal.

Svava currently holds around 18.8% stake in SelfWealth.

SelfWealth's shares rose above 3% in early trading on Monday.

data.title: China Reinsurance Logs 6.63 Billion Yuan in January P&C Premiums
data.description: China Reinsurance subsidiary China Continent Property & Casualty Insurance reported premiums of approximately 6.63 billion yuan in January, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: PolyNovo 1H Net A$3.3M Vs. Net A$2.7M >PNV.AU
Strandline Resources Enters Voluntary Administration After Failure to Secure Funding for Working Capital Amid Debt Restructuring
data.description:
Strandline Resources said McGrathNicol Restructuring appointed Rob Brauer, Linda Smith, and Jason Preston as the company's receivers and managers, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The appointments follow the company's decision to appoint Thomas Birch and Jeremy Nipps from Cor Cordis as voluntary administrators after failing to secure funding for working capital amid a debt restructuring process, a separate filing showed.

The receivers have assumed control of the company's operations and intend to continue operating on a "business as usual" basis while a sale and recapitalization or either process is undertaken, the filing said.

The first meeting of creditors is expected to be on March 6, per the filing.

data.title: Railtel Corporation shares in focus on order win worth Rs 288 crore from East Central Railway
data.description:
Railtel Corporation of India share price will remain in focus on February 24 after company received the work order from East Central Railway for works amounting to Rs 2,88,14,67,426 (Including Tax).

The work order includes provision of Kavach (indigenous Train Collision Avoidance System) on low density railway track in 502.2 RKm of East Central Railway, which is to complete by February 20, 2027.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

On February 20, company won work order from High Court of J&K And Ladakh for service order amounting to Rs 22,44,28,034.

On February 12, company received the work order from State Project Director (Spd) Bihar Education Project Council (Bepc) for supply amounting to Rs 16,97,44,575 and also received the work order from State Project Director (Spd) Bihar Education Project Council (Bepc) for supply, installation and training for operationalization of ISM Labs for BEPC schools amounting to Rs. 69,20,72,766.

On February 6, the company bagged a work order from North Frontier Railway for provision of video surveillance system amounting to Rs 49,67,96,195.

In the quarter ended December 2024, the company posted nearly 5 percent on year growth in net profit to Rs 65 crore, up from Rs 62.10 crore in the year ago period.Revenue was also up 14.8 percent year-on-year to Rs 767.6 crore, as against Rs 668.4 crore in Q3 FY24.

data.title: Updating the USD 'battle of the trendlines'
data.description:
I posted last month on what appeared, to me, to be the end of the US dollar uptrend:

  • The steep up trend line for the USD appears to have been broken

One of the traps that some folks fall into is that if an uptrend breaks then we must be in a downtrend.

Don't ask me how I know this trap ;-)

In that post a month ago I cautioned against assuming a downtrend, and even pointed out a horizontal support line the dollar was resting on:

  • I am suspecting that while the steep uptrend is over, it'll faf around for a while before establishing its next direction.

Which is what we got.

But now it looks weaker, pic below. BTW, I also pointed out in that linked post above (you really should check it out) that first year of Trump's Presidency #1 was a downtrend for the USD. Here we go again?

I'm sure ForexLive traders out there will have better ideas on this me, post 'em in the comments please!

data.title: Gold Hovers Near Record High
data.description:
Gold fell below $2,930 per ounce on Monday but remained close to all-time highs, supported by strong safe-haven demand.

The precious metal continued to find support amid growing concerns over U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs plans, which could escalate global trade tensions.

On the economic front, reports on Friday showed US business activity slowed and consumer confidence waned, further boosting gold’s appeal as a hedge against economic uncertainty.

Investor focus will be on the PCE index, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation metric, due on Friday, which could offer more clues about the central bank’s interest rate trajectory.

Elsewhere, physical gold demand in China and India remained weak, as record-high prices deterred buyers.

data.title: MISC Raised to Buy From Hold by Maybank IB >3816.KU
House Foods Group Revises Financial Forecast, Anticipates Extraordinary Loss
data.description:
House Foods Group expects extraordinary loss in the fourth quarter of the fiscal year ending March 31, leading to a revision of its consolidated financial forecast, according to its filing last Friday.

The impairment loss, approximately 4.6 billion yen, stems from Keystone Natural Holdings' reduced profitability in the US tofu and plant-based foods market, driven by changes in consumer behavior due to a cost-of-living crisis.

In response, the company reorganized its US tofu business in January 2025, integrating strategic and sales functions into House Foods Holding USA for improved profit and loss management.

The revised forecast include net sales of 316 billion yen (from 320 billion yen previously) and profit attributable to owners of parent at 12.3 billion yen (from 13.3 billion yen initially).

Despite these adjustments, the company's dividend forecast remains unchanged.

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar stronger on Monday
data.description:
The Thai baht strengthened against the U.S. dollar on Monday.

At 0210 GMT, the baht was 0.21% higher at 33.47 per dollar, after trading in a range of 33.470 to 33.540. It ended the previous session at 33.53 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

data.title: Shoucheng hits over 1-1/2-year peak on investing in China's robot firm
data.description:
** Shares of Shoucheng Holdings , which manages private funds and operates car parking asset, jump 10.1% to HK$1.97, their highest since July 2023, and on course for third consecutive session of gains

** Shoucheng says on Monday its indirectly managed Beijing Robot Industry Development Investment Fund has invested in X Square Robot Technology (Jinan) which focuses on R&D of embodied generalist model for application of general-purpose robots

** Company says it will continue to increase its investment in the robot industry

** The Hang Seng Composite Index on information technology firms (.HSCIIT) and the Hang Seng Tech Index slip 0.5% and 0.1% respectively, while Hang Seng Index adds 0.4%

** Stock up 76.9% YTD

data.title: China Reinsurance Forecasts 80% to 100% Rise in 2024 Profit
data.description:
China Reinsurance forecasts its profit for 2024 will rise 80% to 100% from 5.65 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The foreseen rise in profit is attributable to business expansion, higher underwriting profit, and prudent investments, according to the filing.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Sunday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Stock Futures Rise as Exit Polls Indicate Conservatives Win Germany's Elections

Stocks ended last week on a down note on concerns about a weakening consumer.

What Do Mass Federal Layoffs Mean for the Labor Market?

The economic impact of federal cutbacks will depend on how many jobs are actually cut and the spillover to contractors and spending.

Warren Buffett Occasionally Writes to Shareholders About Overheated Markets. Not This Year.

The Berkshire Hathaway chairman acknowledged that "often, nothing looks compelling" in the hunt for equity investments.

The 'next important test for AI bulls' happens this week with Nvidia's earnings

Nvidia's profit "could have enough substance even if less sizzle," BofA analysts say.

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. PCE Data, German Elections in Focus

Focus for the week will center on U.S. PCE inflation data and any further news on President Trump's plans for trade tariffs.

European Leaders Draft Peacekeeping Plan for Ukraine With U.S. Forces as 'Backstop'

French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer head to Washington as Europe designs a force to deter Russia.

Ukraine's Zelensky Wants Better Terms on Minerals Deal Demanded by Trump

The Ukrainian president called for security guarantees and better financial terms.

Home Sales Fell 4.9% in January, Extending Slump in Housing Market

Home prices and mortgage rates, which were a drag on home sales for most of 2024, stayed elevated and put off many buyers.

Trump Paralyzes the U.S. Wind Power Industry

The president, who despises wind turbines, has paused federal permits and leasing for such projects, putting company plans in limbo.

U.S. Consumer Confidence Falls Back on Fears of Tariff-Induced Price Increases

Inflation expectations jumped to 4.3% this month from 3.3% in January, the highest reading since 2023.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Sunday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
How Porsche, Volvo and Rivian Are Injecting Unique Driving Styles Into Self-Driving Cars

The automakers are using software and AI to make vehicles of tomorrow more collaborative, working alongside passengers.

Olam Sells Remaining Stake in Agri Products Business to Saudi Arabia's Salic

The Singapore-based company expects to raise $2.58 billion from the sale of its remaining stake in its agricultural products business to Saudi Agricultural and Livestock Investment Company.

How the Internet Made In-Store Shopping Miserable

Physical stores don't have enough staff. Now, they also don't have enough stuff.

Why the Nippon-U.S. Steel Deal Still Has a Chance of Getting Done

As unlikely as it might seem, a victory by the Japanese company can't be ruled out.

A Test for Apple's New iPhone: Beating Chinese Rivals With Home-Field Edge

Apple hopes the new device will allow it to become more competitive in China after the iPhone lost its spot as China's bestselling smartphone.

Khosla Ventures Hits Fundraising Trail

Vinod Khosla's firm Khosla Ventures, a leading backer of artificial intelligence and other startups, has started reaching out to potential investors for its next set of funds, according to people familiar with the situation.

DOJ Investigates Medicare Billing Practices at UnitedHealth

The Justice Department is probing diagnoses and practices that triggered extra payments to UnitedHealth's Medicare Advantage plans, people familiar with the matter say.

FDA Declares Shortage of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, Ozempic Over

The FDA announcement confirms that the U.S. supply of both prescription-only drugs now meets or exceeds the current and projected demand in the country, the Danish pharmaceutical company said.

Apple Pulls Advanced Data Protection Service in U.K.

The service will no longer be available to new users and current U.K. users will need to eventually disable the security feature, Apple said.

Rivian keeps promise on making a profit, but here's why the stock is falling

data.title: Alibaba's ADRs Target Price Rasied to $172.00 From $130.00 by Nomura>BABA
Singapore Exchange Shares Rise After Central Bank Unveils Measures to Boost Equities Market
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui

Singapore Exchange shares rose Monday morning after the country's central bank unveiled measures aimed at boosting the local equities market.

Shares were recently 6.25% higher at 13.60 Singapore dollars, equivalent to US$10.18. Earlier Monday, SGX shares rose as much as 8.4% to S$13.88, the highest intraday level since Feb. 12.

The central bank's package features some tax incentives laid out in the 2025 budget earlier this month, the Monetary Authority of Singapore said after market close last Friday. The measures also seek to streamline public listing processes to make Singapore's equities market more competitive.

The measures were announced by a review group set up by the central bank last year to boost the local market's appeal to investors and companies seeking to list and access growth capital.

The new measures could drive significant inflows into Singapore's equity market, Citi Research economist Wei Zheng Kit said in a note.

"While the timing of these inflows remains uncertain, the potential scale suggests a meaningful impact on market liquidity, particularly benefiting mid-cap and high-yield stocks, with mandates likely focusing on broadening the market," the economist said.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com

data.title: Dollar Falls as Euro Strengthens
data.description:
The dollar index dropped 0.4% to around 106.2 on Monday, reaching an 11-week low as the euro surged following the conservative victory in Germany’s general election.

Friedrich Merz, leader of Germany’s conservative opposition, is now expected to form a coalition government aimed at pushing through fiscal reforms. The dollar has also been under pressure amid growing concerns about the US economy, which could prompt the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates further.

Data released on Friday revealed that US business activity nearly stagnated in February, with services sector contraction contributing to the slowdown.

Consumer sentiment also weakened, driven by inflation worries and the ongoing impact of President Trump’s tariffs.

Traders are now focused on this week’s PCE price index report, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation measure, and a second estimate of fourth-quarter GDP growth.

data.title: Colab Cloud Platforms Forms New Wholly-Owned Subsidiary
data.description:
Sports technology company Colab Cloud Platforms has incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary company named 'Colab Sports Ventures' after receiving a certificate of incorporation from the Central Registration Centre, Ministry of Corporate Affairs, India, according to a Saturday filing to the Indian sock exchanges.

The new company will deal in sports goods equipment and apparels through online and off line channels.

data.title: Chinese Shares Mostly Lower as Investors Await Further Policy Signals — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares are mostly lower in early Asian trading, with the Shanghai Composite Index edging 0.1% lower at 3376.44. The Shenzhen Composite Index is flat at 2089.23 and the ChiNext Price Index is down 0.6%. Investors await for more policy signals in the upcoming Two Sessions. Among major stocks, energy stocks are leading the declines, with PetroChina down 0.9% and China Petroleum & Chemical Corp shedding 0.3%. Technology companies are leading the gains, with iFlytek rising 4.3% and Foxconn Industrial Internet adding 2.6%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Paradigm Biopharmaceuticals Secures Ethics Committee Approval for Phase Three Knee Osteoarthritis Clinical Trial
data.description:
Paradigm Biopharmaceuticals received centralized Human Research Ethics Committee approval in Australia for its pivotal PARA_OA_012 phase three clinical trial, which will study the safety and efficacy of injectable pentosan polysulfate sodium for the treatment of knee osteoarthritis, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

It said the recruitment of the first subject for the trial is on target and first participant dosing is expected in the second quarter, the filing said.

The approval allows the trial to proceed across multiple key clinical trial sites in Australia, the filing said.

The first trial sites to be activated are expected to be located in Victoria.

The firm's shares rose 2% in early trading on Monday.

data.title: China's A Shares, H Shares Likely Have More Upside Potential — Market Talk
data.description: Goldman Sachs remains overweight on China's A shares and H shares. Developments in artificial intelligence will continue benefiting H shares from the fundamental and liquidity perspectives, the analysts say, and see room for A shares to catch up and narrow the return gap with H shares. The coming 'Two Sessions' will likely reiterate the Chinese government's expansionary fiscal policy stance with more details for implementation. This could boost A shares more than H shares since retail investors in the mainland tend to react more to policy announcements, they add. H shares could continue being favored as global funds raise their allocation in China, they add. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Crypto exchange eXch denies laundering Bybit’s hacked funds
data.description:
Crypto exchange eXch has denied laundering money for North Korea’s Lazarus Group following a $1.4 billion Bybit hack on Feb. 21. 

In a Feb. 23 statement to the Bitcointalk forum, the eXch team said the exchange is “Not laundering money for Lazarus/DPRK,” adding that all of its funds were safe and operations unaffected by the Bybit hack.

In a previous post to the forum, the crypto exchange said that anyone stating otherwise is only spreading fear, uncertainty, and doubt (FUD). However, it did admit to processing an “insignificant portion of funds” from the hack. 

“The insignificant portion of funds from the Bybit hack eventually entered our address 0xf1da173228fcf015f43f3ea15abbb51f0d8f1123 which was an isolated case and the only part processed by our exchange, fees from which we will be donated for the public good,” the eXch team said.

“There are no other addresses on the Ethereum blockchain, aside from deposit addresses that interact with this address, that are associated with our exchange,” it added. 

The post was seemingly in response to allegations on social media that it had laundered over $30 million from the hack. 

In a Feb. 22 post to his investigations Telegram group, onchain sleuth ZachXBT said that eXch laundered $35 million of the funds stolen by North Korea’s Lazarus Group from Bybit and then accidentally sent 34 Ether with $96,000 to a hot wallet of another exchange. 

Several other blockchain analysts and the security firm SlowMist have also accused eXch of receiving Ether from wallets associated with the Bybit hack.

Nick Bax, a member of the white hat hacker group the Security Alliance, said that by his “estimate, eXch did about $30M of volume for DPRK today.”

SlowMist also claimed there had been a “significant amount of ETH” converted into other cryptocurrencies on eXch.

Bybit's Feb. 21 hack marks the largest crypto theft in crypto history, with attackers stealing more than $1.4 billion after gaining control of Bybit’s Ether multisig cold wallet.

Bybit continues to process all withdrawals, but its total assets have fallen by over $5.3 billion, according to DefiLlama data, including the $1.4 billion in stolen assets.

Efforts to freeze stolen Bybit funds 

In a Feb. 23 update to X, the exchange said through a “coordinated effort,” over $42 million of the stolen funds had been frozen.

However, Bybit has seemingly met resistance from eXch, according to a forum post from eXch.

In a post to the Bitcointalk forum, the eXch team shared its reply to an email from the Bybit risk team asking them to freeze the funds stolen in the hack. 

The team accused Bybit of freezing some of its users’ funds when they attempted to deposit over the last year, hurting its reputation, and then ghosting all messages sent to resolve the issue.

“In light of these circumstances, we would appreciate a clear explanation as to why we should consider providing assistance to an organization that has actually undermined our reputation,” the eXch team said in the email. 

Commenting on a screenshot of the forum post, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said he hopes “eXch can reconsider and help us to block funds outflowing from them.”

“At this point is really not about Bybit or any entity; it’s about our general approach toward hackers as an industry,” Zhou said.

data.title: Corn slips on expectations of increased US plantings, export demand caps losses
data.description:
Chicago corn futures edged lower on Monday amid expectations that U.S. farmers will respond to a recent price rally by planting more, but strong U.S. export demand limited losses.

Wheat futures also slipped, with snow cover protecting U.S. crops from damage from cold weather. Soybeans rose slightly.

FUNDAMENTALS

* The most-active corn contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) fell 0.1% to $5.04-1/2 a bushel by 0140 GMT after climbing to $5.14 on Friday, highest since August 2023.

* CBOT wheat dropped 0.2% to $6.02-3/4 a bushel, while soybeans added 0.1% to $10.57-3/4 bushel.

* Corn and wheat futures are up around 10% so far this year on expectations of falling stockpiles and tighter supply. Soybeans have risen around 5%, but huge anticipated production in Brazil limited gains.

* Prices have benefited from a weakening U.S. dollar that makes U.S. farm exports more competitive in global markets. The greenback was at its lowest since early December 2024 against a basket of major currencies.

* Export demand for U.S. corn has been strong, with the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) on Friday reporting weekly sales of 1.45 million metric tons, toward the high end of analysts' estimates.

* However, traders think the USDA will project large U.S. corn plantings and yields for the 2025 crop at its annual Agricultural Outlook Forum this week.

* Agricultural lender CoBank projected last week that U.S. corn plantings would rise by around 4% this year as farmers hunt for profit, while that of wheat and soybean would fall.

* Rain forecasts in Argentina's farm belt and parts of Brazil this week also sucked momentum out from corn and soybeans by improving growing conditions.

* Speculators have built a large net long position in CBOT corn and they have historically been in no hurry to unwind such positions, wrote Reuters columnist Karen Braun.

* However, speculators remain bearish on wheat and soybeans.

* In wheat, the condition of crops in France improved slightly but was still close to last year's four-year low, data from farm office FranceAgriMer showed.

MARKETS NEWS

* European shares steadied on Monday as Germany's election produced no nasty surprise, while Wall Street futures firmed on hopes results from AI diva Nvidia this week would justify the tech sector's sky-high valuations.

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Up 0.4%, Reversing Earlier Losses
